message:
feat(ft-xbnl0.2.3): cx-first tx-execution send + wait_for poll (tick 262)

Migrates 3 ambient wezterm calls in the transaction-execution step
runner (`tx_execution.rs`) to their `_with_cx` siblings.

**SendText step action (L325 area, shared `send_cx`):**

- `h.send_text_no_paste(pane_id, &text)` →
  `send_text_no_paste_with_cx(&send_cx, pane_id, &text)`
- `h.send_text(pane_id, &text)` →
  `send_text_with_cx(&send_cx, pane_id, &text)`

Both branches of the no-paste if/else now share a single `send_cx`
binding resolved once at the top of the block_on async scope.

**WaitFor step action (L382 area):**

- `h.get_text(target_pane, false)` →
  `get_text_with_cx(&wait_cx, target_pane, false)` — inside the
  poll loop that re-checks pane output every 200ms until pattern
  match or deadline. A cx-cancel during a long wait_for now cleanly
  aborts each poll iteration instead of running out the full timeout.

All three sites resolve cx via `Cx::current().unwrap_or_else(for_request)`
inside the per-step `std::thread::spawn(...)` + `block_on` wrapper.
Each tx step spawns its own thread with a fresh current_thread runtime,
so cx resolves against whatever the async scope provides.

The tx-execution layer is the async driver for the plan compiler's
StepAction dispatch — migrating it makes mid-transaction cancellation
cleanly propagate into both the send and wait-for wezterm RPC layers.

Verification: `cargo check -p frankenterm --bin ft` compiles clean.

Cumulative ft-xbnl0.2.3 ticks this session: 76 (187-262).
frankenterm-core internal sites: 38 (runtime 7 + mcp 2 + tui 8 + web 4
+ mcp_helpers 2 + mcp_tools 10 + workflows/runner 1
+ workflows/account_steps 1 + tx_execution 3).
main.rs cross-crate total: 127 sites (unchanged this tick).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
